title: Chisocheton longistipitatus
taxonomy: Chisocheton longistipitatus is classified in the family Rutaceae. It belongs to the genus Chisocheton, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. Chisocheton longistipitatus is an evergreen tree or shrub found in the forests of Southeast Asia. The classification of Chisocheton longistipitatus at the order level is in the order Sapindales, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the soapberry order. Chisocheton longistipitatus is further classified at the kingdom level as a part of the Plantae kingdom.

intro: Chisocheton longistipitatus, also known as the Chisocheton tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical forests of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Chisocheton longistipitatus include flavonoids, coumarins, and limonoids, which have been shown to possess antiviral, anti-cancer, and anti-diabetic activities. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal disorders.
